rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Respondents Gary B. Pruitt, Leroy Barnes, Theodore R. "Ted" Mitchell, Jean And Andrew Coblentz As Co Trustees Of The Coblentz 1990 Revocable Trust, William Mcclatchy, Kevin Mcclatchy And Philip Shatz As Executor Of The Estate Of William "Ellery" Mcclatchy.
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the deceased trustees for petitioner to plead facts that would avoid the bar of the statute of limitations. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. The Court cannot conclude on demurrer that respondents exercised their absolute discretion in good faith, and while some aspects of claims may be barred by applicable statutes of limitation, at least some parts of the claims survive a demurrer at least in light of claims being based on the 2010 accounting. Fraudulent concealment is adequately alleged. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 4:30 p.m. Thursday, December 11, 2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondents Gary B. Pruitt, Leroy Barnes, Theodore R. "Ted" Mitchell, Jeand And Andrew Coblentz As Co-Trustees Of The Coblents 1990 Revocable Trust, William Mcclatchy, Kevin Mcclatchy And Philip Shatz As Executor Of The Estate William "Ellery" Mcclatchy To Strike Litigation-Priviledged Correspondence And Punitive Damagae Claims
rulings.Rulings: The motion to strike is denied. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 4:30 p.m. Thursday, December 11, 2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: The Mcclatchy Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. Does receipt of the indemnification agreement in correspondence that petitioner chose not to read constitute willful ignorance or lack of actual knowledge? Are there claims as to which petitioner knew sufficient facts to constitute actual knowledge without regard to the indemnification language and, if so, does that bar use of a Doe amendment upon learning of the indemnification issue?

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Coblentz, Patch, Duffy & Bass, Llp'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint And Notice To Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damage Allegations
rulings.Rulings: Moot lin light of the ruling on the motion to quash. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 4:30 p.m. Thursday, December 11, 2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: The Mcclatchy Company'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Amend Supplemental Petition For Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Trust, Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Trust And Fraud
rulings.Rulings: If the Court reaches the demurrer, the Court is inclined to overrule it. The petition adequately pleads a basis for the company to be interested in Count 1 and an adequate basis for aiding and abetting liability to support overruling the demurrer as to the other counts. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 4:30 p.m. Thursday, December 11, 2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Coblentz,Patch,Duffy,& Bass, Llp'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: The motion to quash is granted. Petititioner knew all the relevant facts from which he now draws his conclusions about the law firm's involvement prior to filing the petition. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) by 4:30 p.m. Thursday, December 11, 2014.
